nyheter.title: 2 defense tech companies sue US Navy after losing out on MUSV program
nyheter.summary: Two defense tech companies sued the U.S. government, alleging they were unfairly excluded from the Navy's Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel program.

nyheter.title: USS Fort Lauderdale Returns from 11-month Deployment to the Caribbean
nyheter.summary: Amphibious transport dock USS Fort Lauderdale (LPD-28) pulled into Naval Station Norfolk on Thursday, capping off a nearly year-long deployment in the Caribbean, the Navy announced. The ship originally deployed as part of the Iwo Jima Amphibious Ready Group and supported missions ranging from the early January raid in Venezuela that captured the country’s former president, Nicolás Maduro, to multiple humanitarian aid and disaster relief response efforts in the region. “During its 334-day deploym

nyheter.title: Blue Water Autonomy, Saildrone launch lawsuits against Navy over MUSV Marketplace
nyheter.summary: Both companies claim that their proposals did satisfy Navy requirements for the new MUSV marketplace.

nyheter.title: Coast Guard Ends Middle East Mission, Moves Cutter Squadron to Western Pacific
nyheter.summary: The U.S. Coast Guard is transitioning its Patrol Forces Southwest Asia (PATFORSWA) to the Western Pacific, ending a more than 20-year forward-deployed mission in the Middle East. The Coast Guard’s Expeditionary Cutter Squadron that makes up PATFORSWA, which was previously forward deployed to Bahrain, will instead operate from Guam and the Pacific Islands, the service announced Wednesday. nyheter.summary: “There’s a wide array of things that we have stopped spending money on so that we could pay for the bills that are associated with Epic Fury,” Gen. Kenneth Wilsbach told Breaking Defense. “That’s why you need a supplemental for this year.”

nyheter.title: UPDATED: Blue Angels Flight Debrief Complete: ‘No Problem’ Says Acting SECNAV
nyheter.summary: No one from the Blue Angels flight demonstration team will be reprimanded or face punishment after Acting Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao said there was “no problem” with a low-altitude pass made Wednesday above Pensacola Beach, Fla. “Flight debrief complete. No reprimands. No firings. No problem. That’s the sound of Freedom! Semper fi and Hooyah,” Cao said Thursday in a post shared to social media platform X alongside a three-second video of the flight. nyheter.summary: Swedish defense firm Saab has secured an 8.7 billion kronor ($900 million) contract to supply combat systems and sensors for the German Navy’s MEKO ships.

nyheter.title: Blue Water Autonomy, Saildrone Sue Navy Over MUSV Rejection
nyheter.summary: Two companies that were passed over for the U.S. Navy’s Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel program are taking the service to court over the decision, USNI News has learned. Saildrone and Blue Water Autonomy filed suit in late June in the United States Court of Federal Claims in Washington, D.C., with two separate complaints alleging the Navy didn’t follow its own rules in how it selected contenders for the first generation of larger autonomous vessels. nyheter.title: Cahill Takes Command of U.S. Naval Surface Force
nyheter.summary: Vice Adm. Joe Cahill took over as the Navy’s top surface warfare officer earlier this month, USNI News has learned. Cahill, who most recently was the commander of Naval Surface Force Atlantic, took the helm of U.S. Naval Surface Force on July 3, according to a service news release. Cahill succeeds Vice Adm. Brendan McLane, who is retiring after serving as the SWOBOSS since December 2023. The ceremony occurred over the Fourth of July holiday weekend at Naval Amphibious Base Coronado in California

nyheter.title: Taiwan Mobilizes HIMARS, Anti-ship Missiles For Major Defense Drill
nyheter.summary: Taiwan’s most capable missile launchers are being deployed across the island for a five-day defense drill designed to enhance joint operations and develop concepts for targeting enemy aircraft and vessels. nyheter.title: MARAD Ship Suffers Fire in Baltimore
nyheter.summary: Two lifeboats aboard U.S. Maritime Administration’s Charles L. Gilliland caught fire Monday in the Port of Baltimore. Baltimore City Fire Department responded around 11 a.m. to the two-alarm fire, meaning additional resources were needed to control the flames. There were no injuries reported. The cause of the fire is under investigation. Gilliland is one of MARAD’s roll-on, roll-off logistics ships. nyheter.title: Navy awards TOTE $2.2 billion to manage Medium Landing Ships project
nyheter.summary: The Navy awarded TOTE $2.2 billion to manage its medium landing ship construction efforts, with the first vessel scheduled for delivery in fall 2029.

nyheter.title: USS Essex at the Center of Additive Manufacturing Trials During RIMPAC 26
nyheter.summary: USS Essex (LHD-2) is the centerpiece of large-scale additive manufacturing tests during Rim of the Pacific 2026 exercise, as the U.S. Navy and Marine Corps lean on modern machine-made parts to hone self-repair capabilities. RIMPAC 26, which is being held in Hawaii through July 31, brings together military, government, academic, commercial and coalition partners in what the U.S. Navy has described as the largest advanced manufacturing demonstration conducted by the Defense Department. nyheter.title: Carrier USS Gerald R. Ford Starts First Major Post-Deployment Maintenance Period
nyheter.summary: Aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) is now at Norfolk Naval Shipyard for a maintenance period following a nearly 11-month deployment. Ford, the Navy’s newest in-service carrier, arrived last week at Norfolk Naval Shipyard in Portsmouth, Va., for its planned incremental availability, or PIA, according to a Navy news release. “[T]his marks Gerald R. nyheter.title: History in the Making: Ukraine Conducts First Fully Unmanned Amphibious Raid at Kinburn Split
nyheter.summary:
The Ukrainian armed forces have reportedly conducted a groundbreaking amphibious raid against Russian-held positions at the Kinburn Split. Executed entirely by unmanned vehicles operating across the sea, land, and air domains, the operation marks the first completely automated amphibious assault in military history conducted under wartime conditions. nyheter.title: Navy Awards TOTE $2.2B to Serve as Vessel Construction Manager for Landing Ship Medium
nyheter.summary: The Navy on Monday issued TOTE Services a $2.2 billion contract to function as the vessel construction manager for the Marine Corps’ Landing Ship Medium program, according to a Defense Department contract announcement. The award is worth up to $2.6 billion with options for as many as eight McClung-class LSMs. Gulf Coast-based Bollinger Shipyards is building the first LSM, while Wisconsin-based Fincantieri Marinette Marine in April won a $30 million for long lead items for four LSMs. “For t

nyheter.title: U.S. Reinstates Naval Blockade in Strait of Hormuz
nyheter.summary: The U.S. will resume its blockade of Iranian ports, coasts and ships, according to a Monday announcement from U.S. Central Command, signaling an end to the 60-day ceasefire between Iran and Washington. The U.S. lifted its blockade on June 18 as part of the memorandum of understanding between the two countries. The blockade will be reimplemented on Tuesday – less than one month into the ceasefire.
